pattern: Only the total count of 1s and 0s available in t matters, not their original positions, and because a higher bit position always outweighs every lower one combined, a left-to-right greedy assignment (give each s-bit its complementary t-bit while the supply lasts) maximizes the result without ever needing to search rearrangements.
hints: Rearranging t means only the multiset of characters in t matters for the final answer, not where they originally sat — start by counting how many '1's and '0's t has available to hand out.

Work through s from the most significant (leftmost) position to the least. For each position, the best possible local choice is to XOR in a bit that makes this output bit 1 (i.e. the complement of s's bit here), because a 1 in a more significant position always beats any arrangement of the remaining lower positions.

Greedily consume from the matching complementary supply (ones or zeros) whenever it's available; when it runs out, you're forced to place the other kind, producing a 0 at that position. Because higher positions are always resolved first and greedily, this single left-to-right pass with two counters produces the true maximum, no backtracking needed.

edge_inputs.description: single matching zero bits — XOR of 0 and 0 is 0
single matching one bits — XOR of 1 and 1 is 0
all zeros in both — no rearrangement can create a 1 anywhere
s is all ones, t is all zeros — every position is forced to 1 regardless of rearrangement (only one arrangement of t exists)
s is all zeros but t has exactly one '1' available — only one position can become a 1, the rest stay 0
t has exactly enough 1s and 0s to perfectly complement every bit of s — the supply is exactly balanced, not oversupplied
s is all ones but t's complementary supply (zeros) runs out one short of covering every position — the last (least significant) position is forced to 0
minimum length (n=1), mismatched bits already giving the max possible single-bit XOR

stress.description: random equal-length binary strings up to a few hundred characters, seeded, exercising many ones/zeros supply balances
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s, t]

oracle_python:
from itertools import permutations

class Solution:
    def maximumXor(self, s: str, t: str) -> str:
        # Independent approach: literally try every distinct rearrangement of t
        # (small n only -- this oracle is exercised on small literal inputs) and
        # keep whichever XOR string is numerically largest.
        n = len(t)
        best = None
        seen = set()
        for perm in permutations(t):
            if perm in seen:
                continue
            seen.add(perm)
            xor_str = ''.join('1' if s[i] != perm[i] else '0' for i in range(n))
            val = int(xor_str, 2)
            if best is None or val > best[0]:
                best = (val, xor_str)
        return best[1]
